interactions.summary: A student continues reading the strategy and Jiang stops on 'peace through strength,' defining it as hegemonic peace: America becomes so mighty and feared that others obey, and obedience produces peace.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Student reading official text; Jiang interrupts to interpret.

interactions.summary: The strategy says America is not isolationist but focused and realistic; Jiang translates that as concentration on core strategic interests, divide-and-rule, then has a student read the four strategic interests.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Student reading official strategy with Jiang commentary.

interactions.summary: Jiang interprets the four interests: rebuild the defense industrial base, turn allies into vassals, contain China through Japan and economic strangulation, and treat the Western Hemisphere as American property.

speaker_notes.note: These are official strategy passages read aloud by students at Jiang's request.

claims.claim: Jiang defines 'peace through strength' as hegemony: America creates peace by being feared, and fear produces obedience.
He says the strategy is not isolationism but focused divide-and-rule around core American strategic interests.
claims.temporal_scope: Reading of the 2026 National Defense Strategy.

claims.claim: Jiang reads burden-sharing as vassalage: Japan, South Korea, Europe, and NATO are no longer friends but subordinate partners ordered to protect U.S. power.
claims.temporal_scope: Interpretation of the strategy in the 2026-04-21 lecture.

claims.claim: He says America's China strategy is not to destroy China but to make China obey through economic strangulation, Japanese balancing, and U.S. sales of resources, weapons, and financing to both sides.
He interprets defending the homeland and Western Hemisphere interests as the claim that the Western Hemisphere belongs to America and outsiders need U.S. permission to trade there.

signature_moments.moment: Peace is not harmony; it is what happens when everyone is afraid enough to obey.
signature_moments.source_phrase: When people fear us, that's when people will obey us
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is Jiang's core translation of the strategy's moral language into imperial mechanics.

signature_moments.moment: Burden-sharing becomes a system of vassalage, where allies are told they are no longer friends but subjects.
signature_moments.source_phrase: from now on, we're not friends. From now on, we're the boss
signature_moments.why_it_matters: The sentence turns a bland policy term into a feudal map of U.S. alliance management.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang's definition: hegemonic peace produced by fear, invincibility, and obedience.
Jiang's term for allies and partners being subordinated to U.S. strategic command.

chronology_notes.note: The lecture ties its China and Western Hemisphere model directly to the four strategic interests of the 2026 strategy.
